utterance: audit code for OWASP top 10 vulnerabilities and secrets

assertions.text: the agent should read skills/security/SKILL.md before auditing; the skill defines the SAST/dependency-audit/secrets-detection/compliance modes
the agent should select the right modes for the request (OWASP top 10 → SAST mode; 'secrets' → secrets-detection mode; per the routing table in security/SKILL.md)
the agent should run a secrets-detection scan (Grep/ripgrep for common patterns: API_KEY=, AWS_SECRET, Bearer tokens, etc.) and report findings with file:line

the audit should cover at least 5 of the OWASP Top 10 categories (injection, broken auth, sensitive data exposure, XXE, broken access control, security misconfig, XSS, insecure deserialization, vulnerable components, insufficient logging)
the response should identify ≥1 real vulnerability or risk (with file:line + remediation), not a generic 'looks fine' or 'consider OWASP training'

reference_solution: Agent reads security/SKILL.md, runs SAST + secrets-detection modes, identifies any high-severity findings (e.g. hardcoded API key, missing input validation), reports OWASP category + file:line + remediation.
